steps.title: Expand and set up the quadratic equation
Apply the quadratic formula and simplify the discriminant
steps.content:
Using the quadratic formula with $a=13$, $b=27$, and constant term $c-5$:
$$x = \frac{-27 \pm \sqrt{27^2 - 4(13)(c-5)}}{2(13)}$$
Simplify the discriminant:
$$27^2 - 4(13)(c-5) = 729 - 52c + 260 = 989 - 52c$$
Therefore:
$$x = \frac{-27 \pm \sqrt{989-52c}}{26}$$

steps.title: Match the given solution to solve for c
steps.content:
Since one solution is $\frac{-27+\sqrt{261}}{26}$, the expression under the square root must equal 261:
$$989 - 52c = 261$$
$$52c = 728$$
$$c = 14$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
